entries.what_they_read: Saw the ticker scrolling $LBJ $KD $SGA and the big 'Claim $100 →' button first, then the three-step breakdown. Caught 'devnet SOL' and 'no real money' somewhere in the fine print.
entries.their_interpretation: It's basically a fantasy stock market for NBA players but built on Solana — fake money, real player stats driving prices, and if you call the market wrong you get rekt in play money. Vibes like a paper trading sim but with that crypto token wrapper I actually fw.
entries.their_rationale: No wallet, no seed phrase, no signup — that's literally zero friction and zero risk, so why not. The ticker with real player tickers scrolling LIVE got me, feels like something's actually moving even if it's devnet.
confusion_notes: 'oracle updates' — what oracle, how often, who sets it?
'fair-value price' vs 'market price' gap — sounds clean in theory but I wanna see actual numbers moving before I trust the model
'0.667 SOL · $100' — why is that the conversion shown? Is devnet SOL pegged to something or is that just a display number?
Badges say 'preview-only for Demo 1' — so I can't actually earn them yet? Then why show them at all rn
first_impression_elements: scrolling live ticker with NBA player tickers ($LBJ, $KD, $SGA) — felt like a real exchange for a second
big 'Claim $100 →' button — obvious, clean, no sketchy vibes
'No wallet yet · no real money · no seed phrase' tagline under the button — removed all my hesitation instantly
ignored_elements: the full paragraph explaining how buying raises price for the next person
'How it works' anchor link — already read the steps inline so didn't need to click

checkpoint.q1_what_am_i_buying: It's like a token for a player, LeBron or whoever, fake money stuff on Solana devnet. Not real shares or anything, just a pretend position that's worth something if the number goes up.
checkpoint.q2_what_moves_price: Buying pushes it up, selling pushes it down — that part's clear. Stats do something to a 'fair value' price too but honestly I'm just watching for green candles and momentum from other people buying.
checkpoint.q3_hold_week: No idea exactly, the app mentioned 'oracle updates' a few times so I guess the fair value price changes on some schedule? But I don't know how often that is or if holding does anything special besides maybe unlocking that Diamond Hands badge.
checkpoint.q4_fair_vs_market: Fair value is what the computer thinks the player's worth based on stats, market price is what people are actually paying. Gap between them is supposed to be the play — but honestly I'm not sweating the math, if it's green I'm in.

first_impression_elements: Orange 'real stats.' text hits hard — pops against the dark background immediately
Live ticker scrolling across the top with green dot — looks like actual market data, got my attention
Big yellow 'Claim $100' button — that's the first thing I clicked mentally
ignored_elements: The grid background texture — didn't register at all
The 'NEW HERE? 10-SECOND VERSION' explainer block at the bottom — too much reading
The 'How it works' secondary CTA — why would I read that when there's free money
data.visual_notes: Dark mode crypto aesthetic is clean enough, feels like a legit trading terminal lite. The ticker bar up top makes it look alive which is good. But below the fold it gets sparse and empty — too much dead black space between the hero and the explainer, makes it feel unfinished, like a devnet project (which it literally says it is, which doesn't help trust).
